codeReview.summary: The worker implemented a targeted fix for VAL-INTEGRATION-007 (cross-origin request ID correlation) by adding X-Request-ID to CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers and adding Access-Control-Expose-Headers. The implementation is correct, minimal, and properly verified. However, the feature's scope was very broad (11 VAL-INTEGRATION assertions covering routes, error codes, response shapes, websocket lifecycle, etc.) but only one specific gap was addressed. The existing openclaw-validation-report.md shows most assertions were already satisfied by prior work - the worker correctly identified and fixed the remaining gap but should have clarified this scope interpretation in the handoff.

issues.description: CORS configuration (Access-Control-Allow-Headers, Access-Control-Expose-Headers) is not documented in openclaw-manifest.json or any docs files. Cross-origin clients need to know which headers are allowed/exposed. Recommend adding a CORS section to the manifest or api-reference.md.

No automated tests added for CORS preflight behavior or X-Request-ID in expose headers. Manual curl verification was performed but not captured in tests. The existing TestRequestIDMiddlewareAddsHeader and TestRequestIDMiddlewarePropagatesExistingID test the request ID middleware but not the CORS layer.

Feature scope clarification needed in handoff: The feature description covers 11 VAL-INTEGRATION assertions (routes, error codes, response shapes, websocket lifecycle, etc.) but the handoff only addresses VAL-INTEGRATION-007. The handoff's 'whatWasLeftUndone' is empty, implying completeness, but the other 10 assertions appear to have been satisfied by prior work (per openclaw-validation-report.md showing all checks pass). The handoff should have explicitly stated that the other assertions were already satisfied.

sharedStateObservations.observation: CORS middleware configuration is not documented in the OpenClaw manifest or API documentation. The implementation allows 'Authorization, Content-Type, X-Request-ID' as request headers and exposes 'X-Request-ID' in responses, but this isn't captured in client-facing docs.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: docs/openclaw-manifest.json has no 'cors' section; docs/ directory has no files mentioning 'Access-Control' or 'CORS'; internal/server/server.go:397-403 sets these headers in corsMiddleware()

sharedStateObservations.observation: The docs/openclaw-validation-report.md provides excellent documentation of the manifest-to-implementation alignment work done previously. This is a good example of documenting validation results for future reference.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: docs/openclaw-validation-report.md shows comprehensive checklist of route/auth/response shape validation with resolution status for previously reported gaps

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed go-integration-worker skill procedure correctly: read docs, identified the specific integration gap (VAL-INTEGRATION-007), made targeted change, ran validators (make test, make lint, go build), and performed manual verification on port 8080. No skill procedure gaps identified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows skill invocation → doc reading → test/lint runs → manual curl verification → commit with co-authorship format

summary: PASS with minor observations. The CORS fix for cross-origin request ID correlation (VAL-INTEGRATION-007) is correctly implemented with a minimal 3-line change. All validators pass. The implementation enables browser clients to send and read X-Request-ID headers for request correlation. Minor gaps: (1) CORS configuration should be documented in the OpenClaw manifest or API docs, (2) handoff should have clarified that the other 10 VAL-INTEGRATION assertions were already satisfied by prior work rather than implying the broad feature scope was fully addressed in this session.
